title: support label cleanup: TIER service + drop custom Opened field
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Two small support-project cleanups.

## 1. TIER as a SERVICE label
Adds `TIER` to the support SERVICE label set so the classifier can apply it when a user asks about their tier (spore/seed/flower/nectar/upgrade). Treated identically to IMAGE/TEXT/AUDIO/etc. — exactly one SERVICE per issue.

(Unrelated to `TIER-APP-*`, which is the app-submission workflow.)

- `VALID_LABELS` + `SUPPORT_SERVICE_LABELS` include TIER
- prompt lists TIER under SERVICE
- TRIAGE.md SERVICE table includes TIER
- backfill's remove-set + `has_project_labels` heuristic include TIER

## 2. Drop the custom "Opened" date field
GitHub Projects v2 now has built-in Created/Updated date columns, making the custom Opened field redundant.

- remove `opened_field_id` from CONFIG (Dev + Support)
- remove `set_date_field` and its call site in `project-manager.py:main`
- strip the date pipeline from `project-backfill-labels.py` (GraphQL fragment, `--skip-dates` flag, `needs_date` branch, `ISSUE_CREATED_AT`)

## Out-of-band after merge
- Delete the "Opened" custom field from the Dev and Support project boards in the GitHub Projects UI.

gist.headline: Tidying the triage board
gist.blurb: Cleaned up our support issue classifier to better handle tier-related questions and dropped some redundant date fields.
gist.summary: Added a new TIER service label to the support issue classifier and removed the custom Opened date field in favor of GitHub's built-in columns.
gist.impact: Internal project boards are cleaner and the automated triage system can now properly categorize questions about user tiers.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing files or labels on a retro computer monitor. A cute robot helper holding a 'TIER' label. Cozy office/workspace setting.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot organizing colorful pixelated folders on a retro computer terminal. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds up a glowing lime green (#ecf874) label that says 'TIER'. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm and nostalgic.
